definition: With no qualification; totally confident.
State as a fact strongly and publicly.
State a fact or belief confidently and forcefully.
Tell someone something positively to dispel any doubts.
Confidently; with certainty.
Able to be trusted as being accurate or true.
In a way that is unambiguously explicit and direct.
Undoubtedly; definitely.
Evoking interest, attention, or admiration in a powerfully irresistible way.
In a way that is achieved easily or by a large margin.
Be of the same opinion; agree.
In a self-assured way that expresses faith in oneself.
Establish the truth or correctness of something previously believed.
In every case or on every occasion; invariably.
Assert something as a position in an argument.
Cause someone to believe firmly in the truth of something.
Confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding.
Decisive or critical, especially in the success or failure of something.
In a decisive and confident way.
Say something in a solemn and emphatic manner.
Regard or consider in a specified way.
Without doubt.
Done or reached decisively and with authority.
Decisively and with authority; conclusively.
State or order authoritatively.
Certainly; without doubt.
Give special importance or prominence to something in speaking or writing.
In a forceful way.
Make certain that something shall occur or be the case.
Absolutely necessary; extremely important.
Show to be true or certain by determining the facts.
With little possibility of movement; securely.
Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance.
Provide a formal assurance or promise, especially that certain conditions shall be fulfilled relating to a product, service, or transaction.
Not able to be denied or disputed.
In a way that cannot be challenged or denied.
Without a doubt; impossible to doubt.
Certain to happen; unavoidable.
As is certain to happen; unavoidably.
Demand something forcefully, not accepting refusal.
Direct or command someone to do something, especially as an official order.
In every case or on every occasion; always.
State something strongly to be the case; assert.
With certainty; so as to leave no room for doubt.
Suggest or assume the existence, fact, or truth of something as a basis for reasoning, discussion, or belief.
To a profound extent; extremely.
Put forward a plan or suggestion for consideration or action.
Demonstrate the truth or existence of something by evidence or argument.
Say or do something to remove the doubts and fears of someone.
Say something again or a number of times, typically for emphasis or clarity.
Dismiss as inadequate, inappropriate, or not to one's taste.
Settle or find a solution to a problem, dispute, or contentious matter.
In an admirably purposeful, determined, and unwavering manner.
Express something definitely or clearly in speech or writing.
With great power or force.
Provide evidence to support or prove the truth of.
Used to emphasize the speaker's firm belief that what they are saying is true and often their surprise that there is any doubt of this.
In a way that cannot be denied or disputed.
Without doubt; certainly.
In a way that leaves no doubt.
In a way that cannot be disputed or doubted; without question.
Check or prove the validity or accuracy of something.
Make sure or demonstrate that something is true, accurate, or justified.
